heading: Omitted
label: 681 to 689 Omitted
notes: CodificationSection 681, , , , stated purpose of this chapter as establishment and maintenance of a program of stabilization payments (which terminated ) to small domestic producers of lead and zinc ores and concentrates in order to stabilize the mining of lead and zinc by such producers. See note for section 687 below.Pub. See note for section 687 below.Pub. L. 87–347, § 275 Stat. 766Pub. See note for section 687 below.Pub. L. 87–347, § 375 Stat. 767Pub. L. 89–238, § 1(2)79 Stat. 925Oct. 3, 1961Oct. 5, 1965December 31, 1969Section 684, , , , authorized Secretary to promulgate such regulations and require such reports as deemed necessary to carry out program of stabilization payments (which terminated ) under this chapter. See note for section 687 below.Pub. L. 87–347, § 475 Stat. 767Oct. 3, 1961December 31, 1969Section 685, , , , authorized Secretary to delegate functions relating to stabilization payments (which terminated ) under this chapter to Administrator of General Services. L. 89–238, § 1(3)79 Stat. 925Oct. 3, 1961July 25, 1963Oct. 5, 1965December 31, 1969Section 687, , , ; , , , provided that no payment be made under this chapter after , but permitted authorized payment only if application therefor was filed not later than .Pub. L. 87–347, § 775 Stat. 768Pub. L. 89–238, § 1(4)79 Stat. 925Oct. 3, 1961Oct. 5, 1965Dec. 31, 1969Mar. 31, 1970Section 688, , , , required annual reports to Congress on operations relating to stabilization payments (which terminated ) under this chapter not later than first day of March each year. See note for section 687 above.Pub. L. 87–347, § 875 Stat. 768Oct. 3, 1961December 31, 1969Section 689, , , ; , , , related to penalties for procuring a stabilization payment (which terminated ) not entitled to under this chapter and civil and criminal liability for keeping a payment not entitled to under this chapter.
